summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is absolute value?

Absolute value measures how far a number is from zero, regardless of direction. It is always zero or positive: |5| = 5 and |-5| = 5.

What is an 'absolute value equation'?

An absolute value equation like |ax + b| = c requires splitting into two cases (ax + b = c and ax + b = -c) because two different inputs can produce the same absolute value.

What is an 'absolute value inequality'?

An absolute value inequality like |x| < a converts to -a < x < a (AND), while |x| > a converts to x > a or x < -a (OR).

An elevator has a weight limit modeled by |w - 1500| <= 200, where w is the actual weight capacity (in lbs) that may vary from the target. What is the range of acceptable weights?

-200 <= w - 1500 <= 200. Add 1500: 1300 <= w <= 1700 lbs.

A school bus picks up students. The schedule says the bus arrives at 7:30 AM, but it can vary by up to 5 minutes. Using |t - 7.5| <= 5/60, what is the arrival window in hours?

5 minutes = 5/60 hours. |t - 7.5| <= 5/60 means 7.5 - 5/60 <= t <= 7.5 + 5/60, which is 7:25 AM to 7:35 AM.
